title: Set items, bow mechanics, and deterministic UID
problem: Three gaps: (1) Set items (coordinated multi-piece bonuses) are absent — the highest item tier in D2. (2) Bows are in the loot pool but have no ranged attack mechanic; they behave identically to melee weapons. (3) nextUid uses Math.random(), which can theoretically collide and is not seedable for replay/testing.

lineage.spawnReason: deferred scope + latent bug: set items and bow ranged mechanics were cut from v1; Math.random UID is a correctness risk

analysis: nextUid() used Math.random() making item UIDs non-deterministic. Two items generated from the same RNG seed in the same process could theoretically collide if Math.random() happened to produce the same value. More importantly, mixing seeded RNG (mulberry32) with unseeded Math.random() breaks test reproducibility for any logic that depends on UID ordering. Set items are deferred — UID determinism is the scoped deliverable.
implementation: Replaced Math.random() in nextUid() in src/engine/loot.ts with a module-level counter (_uidCounter) that increments per call and wraps at 0xffffff. UID format: item_{Date.now().toString(36)}_{counter.toString(36).padStart(6,'0')}. The timestamp component still ensures cross-session uniqueness; the counter ensures intra-session uniqueness without Math.random().

decisions.decision: Keep Date.now() in the UID prefix alongside the counter
decisions.rationale: Pure counter would collide across sessions if the store is reloaded and the counter resets. Timestamp prefix provides session isolation.

decisions.decision: Counter wraps at 0xffffff (16M) rather than growing unbounded
decisions.rationale: Keeps UID strings compact. 16M items per session is far above any realistic game session.

decisions.decision: Set items (fixed-stat unique archetypes) are deferred to a future session
decisions.rationale: Set items require a new data layer, display logic, and set-bonus tracking. Scoping the UID fix alone keeps this PR reviewable.

successCriteria: nextUid() no longer calls Math.random(). Two calls within the same millisecond produce distinct UIDs. All 244 engine tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly.
knownLimitations: _uidCounter is module-level state — resets to 0 on module reload (hot reload during dev). Timestamp prefix prevents cross-session collision.
Set items are not implemented in this feature — deferred.
userGuide: Internal change — no player-visible effect. Item identifiers are now collision-resistant without relying on system randomness.
implementationNotes: _uidCounter is declared at module scope with let; & 0xffffff keeps it in safe integer range
Counter is intentionally not exported — UIDs are internal identifiers, not part of the public API

codeSnippets.label: nextUid with counter instead of Math.random()
